headlines.title: Iranian, Russian Crude Premiums Slide as China Pulls Back on Imports
headlines.summary: Weak demand from Iran’s top crude buyer, China, has dragged Iranian flagship oil prices into discounts to ICE Brent for the first time in two months, trade sources told Reuters on Thursday. Demand for Iranian oil in its biggest export market has waned in recent weeks, as imports

headlines.title: India’s Nayara Completes Maintenance at 400,000-Bpd Refinery
headlines.summary: India’s Nayara Energy, which processes Russian oil, has completed a planned turnaround of its 400,000-barrels-per-day refinery at Vadinar, the Indian refiner said on Thursday. headlines.title: Iraq Revives Kurdistan Production in Bid to Rescue Oil Revenues
headlines.summary: Iraq’s Prime Minister Ali Falih Al-Zaidi has directed oil companies operating in the semi-autonomous region of Kurdistan to resume operations beginning on Thursday, as OPEC’s second-largest producer scrambles to export crude with the Strait of Hormuz de facto closed. headlines.title: Pakistan Rushes to Secure LNG as Summer Power Demand Surges
headlines.summary: Pakistan has issued a new tender for LNG, to the amount of 1 million tons, The Nation reported today, citing the seasonal increase in electricity demand as summer heat sets in. This is the fourth LNG tender for the spot market over the last two months as the country grapples with

headlines.title: Oil Prices Dip as Israel-Lebanon Ceasefire Revives Iran Deal Hopes
headlines.summary: Oil prices were under pressure in early Asian trade on Thursday following the announcement of a ceasefire agreement between Israel and Lebanon. The agreement could ultimately help clear the way for a broader U.S.-Iran deal, which would eventually see the Strait of Hormuz reopen a

headlines.title: Europe Scrambles to Contain the Energy Shock
headlines.summary: The European Union and the Eurozone are feeling the energy shock as the Iran war enters its fourth month. The spike in oil and gas prices amid the Middle East crisis is raising inflation and moderating economic growth expectations in the EU and the Euro area, which are grappling

headlines.title: Energy Costs Are Catching Up With Big Tech
headlines.summary: Artificial intelligence is all the rage these days, and so is its energy consumption. headlines.title: A Barrel Trapped Behind Hormuz Isn't Spare Capacity
headlines.summary: At the start of 2026, oil traders were preparing for a glut. Supply growth was expected to outpace demand growth. OPEC+ was gradually returning barrels to the market.
